# home

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `home`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `home.activation` | `DAG of string` | The activation scripts blocks to run when activating a Home Manager generation. Any entry here should be idempotent, meaning running twice or more times produces the same result as running it once. |
| `home.activationGenerateGcRoot` | | |
| `home.activationPackage` | | |
| `home.checks` | `list of package` | Packages that are added as dependencies of the home’s build, usually for the purpose of validating some part of the configuration. |
| `home.emptyActivationPath` | | |
| `home.enableDebugInfo` | `boolean` | Some Nix packages provide debug symbols for gdb in the debug output. This option ensures that those are automatically fetched from the binary cache if available and gdb is configured to find those symbols. |
| `home.enableNixpkgsReleaseCheck` | `boolean` | Determines whether to check for release version mismatch between Home Manager and Nixpkgs. Using mismatched versions is likely to cause errors and unexpected behavior. It is therefore highly recommended to use a release of Home Manager that corresponds with your chosen release of Nixpkgs. |
| `home.extraActivationPath` | | |
| `home.extraBuilderCommands` | | |
| `home.extraDependencies` | `list of path in the Nix store` | A list of paths that should be included in the home closure but generally not visible. |
| `home.extraOutputsToInstall` | `list of string` | List of additional package outputs of the packages home.packages that should be installed into the user environment. |
| `home.extraProfileCommands` | | |
| `home.file` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Attribute set of files to link into the user home. |
| `home.fileOverlapResolution` | | |
| `home.homeDirectory` | `absolute path` | The user’s home directory. Must be an absolute path. |
| `home.keyboard` | `null or (submodule)` | Keyboard configuration. Set to null to disable Home Manager keyboard management. |
| `home.language` | `submodule` | Language configuration. |
| `home.packages` | `list of package` | The set of packages to appear in the user environment. |
| `home.path` | | |
| `home.pointerCursor` | `null or (submodule)` | Cursor configuration. |
| `home.preferXdgDirectories` | `boolean` | Whether to make programs use XDG directories whenever supported. |
| `home.profileDirectory` | `absolute path (read only)` | The profile directory where Home Manager generations are installed. |
| `home.services` | | |
| `home.sessionPath` | `list of string` | Extra directories to prepend to PATH . |
| `home.sessionSearchVariables` | `attribute set of list of string` | Extra directories to prepend to arbitrary PATH-like environment variables (e.g.: MANPATH ). The values will be concatenated by : . These directories are added to the environment variable in a double-quoted context, so expressions like $HOME are expanded by the shell. However, since expressions like ~ or * are escaped, they will end up in the environment verbatim. |
| `home.sessionVariableSetter` | | |
| `home.sessionVariables` | `lazy attribute set of (string or absolute path or signed integer or floating point number)` | Environment variables to always set at login. |
| `home.sessionVariablesExtra` | | |
| `home.sessionVariablesPackage` | | |
| `home.shell.enableBashIntegration` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Bash integration. |
| `home.shell.enableFishIntegration` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Fish integration. |
| `home.shell.enableIonIntegration` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Ion integration. |
| `home.shell.enableNushellIntegration` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Nushell integration. |
| `home.shell.enableShellIntegration` | `boolean` | Whether to globally enable shell integration for all supported shells. |
| `home.shell.enableZshIntegration` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Zsh integration. |
| `home.shellAliases` | `attribute set of string` | An attribute set that maps aliases (the top level attribute names in this option) to command strings or directly to build outputs. |
| `home.stateVersion` | `one of “18.09”, “19.03”, “19.09”, “20.03”, “20.09”, “21.03”, “21.05”, “21.11”, “22.05”, “22.11”, “23.05”, “23.11”, “24.05”, “24.11”, “25.05”, “25.11”, “26.05”` | It is occasionally necessary for Home Manager to change configuration defaults in a way that is incompatible with stateful data. This could, for example, include switching the default data format or location of a file. |
| `home.uid` | `null or (unsigned integer, meaning >=0)` | The user’s uid. |
| `home.username` | `string` | The user’s username. |
| `home.version.full` | | |
